critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
Three confounds: demand-response programs may reduce load forecast difficulty; TOU tariff changes the load process shape; weather vendor switch changes input quality. Year-over-year comparison is not a controlled methodology test. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
Complex ensemble is a genuine design improvement. The utility legitimately operates in the new demand-response environment. But methodology versus environment cannot be disentangled without a matched historical evaluation. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
adjudication_raw:
ASSESSOR 3:
Three assessors agree on empirical_test_agreed.

SYNTHESIS:
Strong consensus. The year-over-year comparison confounds methodology with environment and input quality changes. A matched historical evaluation is required.

ETD SPECIFICATION:
- Measure: MAPE for both models on the same historical summer intervals with identical weather inputs; evaluate both on days without demand-response activation and separately on days with curtailment events
- Success criterion: New model shows no consistent MAPE improvement on days without curtailment events in matched evaluation — confirming the year-over-year error gap reflects demand-response and input changes, not modeling methodology
- Failure criterion: New model shows consistent MAPE improvement of 1+ percentage point on non-curtailment days in matched historical evaluation — confirming genuine methodology advantage

empirical_test.measure: MAPE on matched historical intervals with identical weather inputs; non-curtailment and curtailment day segments evaluated separately
empirical_test.success_criterion: No consistent improvement on non-curtailment days in matched evaluation — confirming environmental and input changes explain year-over-year gap
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Consistent 1pp+ improvement on non-curtailment days in matched evaluation — genuine methodology advantage confirmed
